meta.title: Guangdong
meta.subtitle: Industry-cluster overview
meta.tagline: Guangdong is China's export workshop — the Pearl River Delta plus the Chaoshan east, the seafood-and-petrochemical west, and the mineral north. This is the cluster map of the whole province.
meta.methodology: Coordinates are from Google Places, converted from China's GCJ-02 datum to WGS-84 to sit correctly on the OpenStreetMap / CARTO basemap. Each pin marks a place's dominant manufacturing specialty drawn from current sourcing guides; clusters are indicative, not exclusive, and shift over time. The map covers all 21 of Guangdong's prefecture-level cities; the Pearl River Delta overview maps the delta core in its own right.

clusters.note: Autos (GAC), Panyu jewelry, beauty, and a whole city of wholesale markets.
Leather-goods and handbag capital; Huadu autos nearby.
Electronics and tech capital — Huaqiangbei, 3C, drones (DJI), telecoms (Huawei, ZTE).
China's ceramics capital — building and sanitary ceramics (Shiwan, Chancheng).
Home-appliance capital — Midea and Galanz; the world's appliance workshop.
The world's largest furniture market and a vast furniture cluster.
Aluminium-profile capital; Xiqiao textiles and underwear.
Fashion-apparel capital — vast garment manufacturing and wholesale markets.
Shoe-manufacturing town; also the big furniture-fair grounds.
World electronics-manufacturing hub — phones and components (Oppo, Vivo, Huawei nearby); Dalingshan furniture, Chang'an moulds.
Lighting capital — the world's largest LED-lighting cluster and market.
Hardware and lock capital; acoustic electronics and gas appliances.
Gree air-conditioning HQ; printer consumables (ink, cartridges) and electronics.
Faucet and sanitary-ware capital; bathroom hardware.
Microphone capital — wireless mics and audio gear.
Electronics and smartphones (TCL, Desay); batteries and displays.
New-energy vehicles (Xpeng) and metalworking and hardware.
China's toy capital — the world's largest toy-manufacturing base.
Lingerie and underwear manufacturing hub; knitted garments.
Porcelain capital — daily-use and sanitary ceramics, second only to Foshan.
Wedding-gown and evening-wear capital; Chaozhou embroidery.
Textile and lingerie hub with a major garment market; herbal-medicine trade.
Stainless-steel hardware and kitchenware (Jiedong); plastics.
Toys, electronics and gold jewelry (Haifeng).
Electronics and building materials; Hakka food and pomelo.
Prawn and seafood-processing capital; steel (Baosteel) and petrochemicals.
One of China's great petrochemical bases (Sinopec); lychee.
Knife, scissors and cutlery capital — the world's largest kitchen-knife cluster.
Recycled copper and aluminium hub; ceramics relocated from Foshan.
Steel and heavy industry (Shaoguan Steel); machinery and tobacco.
Stone capital — marble and granite processing.
Mobile-phone parts and electronics; bottled water and rare earth.

orientation.v: The manufacturing core: Shenzhen electronics, Dongguan and Huizhou phones, Foshan ceramics/appliances/furniture, Zhongshan lighting, Guangzhou autos and markets.
Shunde appliances, Lecong furniture, Guzhen lighting, Xiaolan hardware, Humen apparel, Shiling leather, Shuikou faucets — one product per town.
A distinct world: Chenghai toys (the world's biggest), Chaozhou and Fengxi ceramics, Puning and Gurao lingerie and textiles.
Yangjiang knives and cutlery, Zhanjiang seafood and steel, Maoming petrochemicals.
Yunfu stone, Qingyuan recycled metals and ceramics, Shaoguan steel, Heyuan electronics.

notes: Guangdong is China's export workshop — the Pearl River Delta is among the densest manufacturing regions on earth, ringed by distinct clusters in the east, west and mountainous north.
The delta packs single-product towns as tightly as Zhejiang: appliances in Shunde, furniture in Lecong, lighting in Guzhen, hardware in Xiaolan, apparel in Humen, leather in Shiling.
Beyond the delta, Chaoshan in the east is a world of its own (toys, ceramics, lingerie), the west adds knives (Yangjiang) and petrochemicals (Maoming), and the north adds stone (Yunfu) and metals.

A pin marks a place's dominant specialty, not its only one. The Pearl River Delta overview maps the delta core in its own right; this province map extends to all 21 prefectures, and the Shenzhen and Guangzhou pins link to their building-level deep-dives.
